sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: on the plus side. highly useful in cooking because practical culinary value matters here. efficient energy source because that can be a strength in the right context. helps meals actually work because a fat that people can use well matters more than a theoretical one.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next. extremely calorie dense by default because that is the unavoidable category burden. easy to overpour without noticing because practical portion creep is real. not a complete food on its own because even the better fats are still just part of a meal.

closing.summary: Sesame Oil has a genuinely strong overall case, especially highly useful in cooking and efficient energy source. the downsides are there, but extremely calorie dense by default and easy to overpour without noticing do not outweigh the upside. best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best in controlled amounts where fat quality is the main reason to use it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?
